The IBM Q Valencia device then calculated the dipole moments of LiH (and simulated the other three) to understand the electronic distribution in a molecule across its different atoms as bonds are broken.
To make sure our calculations on the hardware were accurate, we also performed them on a classical computer using the IBM quantum simulator, Garcia writes.
Despite working with noisy qubits, we were still able to extract sufficiently precise results.
